channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: S&P 500 and Nasdaq futures rise as Nvidia leads chip stocks higher: Live updates
item.description: April's producer price index, another key inflation report, will be released at 8:30 a.m. on Wednesday morning.

item.title: Jamie Dimon warns JP Morgan may rethink new London office if 'very smart' Starmer is ousted as UK PM
item.description: Starmer's leadership is under pressure, sparking concern in the bond market.

item.title: Alibaba's core profit plunges 84% even as AI and cloud growth accelerate
item.description: Alibaba on Wednesday said its core profitability plunged in the March quarter amid heavy investments in tech and e-commerce.

item.title: Jensen Huang joins Trump's China trip after the U.S. president called the Nvidia CEO
item.description: The U.S. chipmaker executive was not included in earlier lists of business leaders participating in President Donald Trump's trip to China this week.

item.title: Buy these cheaper stocks, Barclays says, as luxury's 'self-help stories' pay off
item.description: The conflict in the Middle East has weighed heavily on the world's biggest luxury stocks, but Barclays sees a buying opportunity.

item.title: CNBC's UK Exchange newsletter: Starmer’s EU push faces a harsh political reality
item.description: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer has alighted on resetting relations with Europe as a way of reviving his flagging popularity.

item.title: Kool-Aid to launch electrolyte packets with no artificial dyes as part of Kraft Heinz makeover
item.description: Parent company Kraft Heinz is trying to reverse slumping sales by modernizing many of its legacy brands.

item.title: Gas tax holiday as Trump promises? Not so fast, trucking, construction industries say
item.description: President Donald Trump and lawmakers are eyeing federal gas tax relief, but opponents argue a pause wouldn't help consumers and could harm roads.

item.title: Marty Makary resigns as FDA commissioner following industry and White House backlash
item.description: Makary's tenure was marked by internal dysfunction at the FDA, leadership turmoil and mounting backlash from drugmakers and physicians on regulatory decisions.

item.title: FedEx CEO brushes off Amazon's new logistics service that recently sent shares tumbling
item.description: FedEx CEO Raj Subramaniam argued that Amazon's recent supply chain services announcement does not pose a threat to Fedex.

item.title: Social Security COLA for 2027 may be higher as inflation rises, new estimates find
item.description: As the pace of inflation increases, that may lead to a higher Social Security cost-of-living adjustment for 2027, according to new expert estimates.

item.title: India hikes bullion import duties as the world's second-largest gold market faces a declining rupee
item.description: The government has imposed a 10% basic customs duty and a 5% tax on gold and silver imports, as per notifications issued on Wednesday.

item.title: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani's pied-à-terre property tax is moving ahead. But will it work?
item.description: New York is moving ahead with a pied-à-terre property tax in its budget. Existing taxes from around the world's major cities show the limits of the policy.

item.title: Companies start getting tariff refunds after Supreme Court decision
item.description: After the Supreme Court ruled some of President Trump's tariffs unconstitutional, the first wave of tariff refunds have begun flowing in.

item.title: Waymo recalls 3,800 robotaxis after glitch allowed some vehicles to 'drive into standing water'
item.description: Waymo issued a voluntary recall of about 3,800 of its robotaxis to fix software issues that could allow them to drive into flooded roadways.

item.title: China's Tencent sees boost from gaming, AI demand even as revenue comes in weaker than expected
item.description: The tech giant reported its first-quarter 2026 earnings on Wednesday.

item.title: SoftBank posts $46 billion gain at Vision Fund driven mainly by massive OpenAI bet
item.description: SoftBank booked a yearly gain of $46 billion at its Vision Fund driven in large part by the huge rise in value of its investment in OpenAI.

item.title: This metal just set a new record, boosted by AI data center demand. Citi says it’s time to 'chase the move higher'
item.description: Citigroup sees copper rising to $15,000 per metric ton, or approximately 7% above the metal's Tuesday close.

item.title: Google races to put Gemini at the center of Android before Apple’s AI reboot
item.description: Google is using its latest Android rollout to position Gemini as the AI layer across phones, Chrome, laptops and cars.

item.title: An under-the-radar AI stock just delivered the best quarter of the chip sector
item.description: Qnity is in a great position to keep grabbing market share, not only as chips get smaller but also as the industry shifts more toward stacking.

item.title: Stellantis’ Leapmotor partnership points to a wider — and riskier— industry gamble
item.description: A recent tie-up between Jeep maker Stellantis and China's Leapmotor is seen as a watershed moment for the future of European carmaking.

item.title: Amex adds first-of-its-kind AI benefit: $300 ChatGPT credit for Business Platinum and Business Gold Cards
item.description: The $300 ChatGPT credit comes with no annual fee change or card benefit adjustments, making it a great addition to your Amex Business Gold or Business Platinum.

item.title: OpenAI trial recap: Altman testifies he never promised Musk to keep company a nonprofit
item.description: Elon Musk is accusing OpenAI CEO Sam Altman and President Greg Brockman of trying to 'steal a charity.'

item.title: Auto-state lawmakers seek to keep certain Chinese vehicles out of U.S. as Trump heads to Beijing
item.description: Michigan lawmakers introduced a bipartisan bill to ban Chinese-connected vehicles and technology from the U.S. market over security concerns.

item.title: Student loan interest rates are set to rise for 2026-27: Expert analysis
item.description: Federal student loan interest rates will rise in the 2026-27 academic year, according to an exclusive analysis provided to CNBC.

item.title: It's not just Iran and oil raising inflation. Prices also are reaccelerating in these other areas
item.description: Here's some of the places where prices are rising fast for consumers.

item.title: Trump doesn't need Congress to restart Iran strikes: Hegseth
item.description: The Iran war, now well into its third month, has caused oil prices to soar globally as Iran continues to lock down the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Trump wants gas tax relief. But slim driver savings could strain a key federal fund
item.description: President Donald Trump floated a federal gas tax suspension. If enacted, here's how that could impact consumers.

item.title: Traders will soon be able to bet on computer chip prices as AI drives costs skyward
item.description: Contracts can be used to hedge against rising GPU rental rates and other operational costs

item.title: Wednesday's big stock stories: What’s likely to move the market in the next trading session
item.description: The S&P 500 pulled back from its record on Tuesday as higher oil prices and a hot consumer inflation report kept stocks under pressure.
